zh.lifeSummary: 當Winifred Nightingale於1580年出生在英國艾可傑爾姆斯福德時，她的父親Miguel Nightingale22歲，母親Ann Haggar20歲。她於1604年10月22日與Thomas Saward在英國艾可格亞特 坎菲爾德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Nightingale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for someone with a good voice, from Middle English nightegale ‘nightingale’ (Old English nihtegale, from niht ‘night’ + galan ‘sing’). The bird is known as a sweet singer.
Probably an Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Nachtigall  , or cognates in other languages.

variants.name: Nachtigall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Arno, Fritz, Guenter, Hedwig, Konrad.
German: nickname from Middle High German nachtegal ‘nightingale’ (from Old High German nacht ‘night ’+ galan ‘to sing’), probably denoting a good singer.

nameMeanings.name: Winifred
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh female personal name Gwenfrewi . The form of the name has been altered by association with   Winfred   and has been in general use among English speakers since the 16th century.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Winifred Nightingale was born in 1580, in Chelmsford, Essex, England, her father, Miguel Nightingale, was 22 and her mother, Ann Haggar, was 20. She married Thomas Saward on 22 October 1604, in Great Canfield, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters.

ja.lifeSummary: Winifred Nightingaleが1580年にイギリスエセックスチェルムスフォードで生まれた時，父Miguel Nightingaleは22歳，母Ann Haggarは20歳であった。1604年10月22日にイギリスエセックスグレアト カンフィールドでThomas Sawardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Winifred Nightingale(이)가 1580년 영국 에세스 헬스퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Miguel Nightingale(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ann Haggar(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1604년 10월 22일 영국 에세스 그리트 캔필드에서 Thomas Saward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다.
